response_headers.note: Headers observed from DevTools Headers tab for completion request
response_headers.content_type: text/event-stream (inferred from EventStream tab presence and h2 protocol)

first_3_events.description: Initial event echoing the user's request message metadata
Response initialization - sets up the response object path
Response field initialization using JSON Patch-like path notation (p=path)

last_2_events.description: Stream close event with UI behavior hints

streaming_token_events.description: Token-by-token content delivery using message events with {v: token} format

sse_protocol_summary.event_naming: Uses named event types: request_message, message, update, title, close
sse_protocol_summary.data_format: JSON objects in each event's data field
sse_protocol_summary.streaming_pattern: JSON Patch-like protocol with 'p' (path) and 'v' (value) fields
sse_protocol_summary.token_delivery: Each token sent as separate 'message' event with {"v": "token_text"}
sse_protocol_summary.completion_signal: Stream ends with update, title, and close events

apf_warning_implications.key_insight: DeepSeek uses named SSE event types (not just 'data:' lines). The frontend likely uses EventSource or similar SSE parser that dispatches on event type.
apf_warning_implications.warning_approach: APF warning template should send SSE events with the correct event types. First event should be 'message' type with a 'v' field containing warning text to appear as streaming content.
apf_warning_implications.critical_events: The 'request_message' event appears first and may be required. The 'close' event signals stream end.

notes: DeepSeek SSE protocol uses a JSON Patch-like structure with named event types. This is different from OpenAI-style SSE which uses unnamed events with 'data: {json}' format. APF warning template must use named event types to be parsed correctly by the DeepSeek frontend.
